Site Plan application for a 44-storey building comprised of 440 residential dwelling units and 223 square meters of retail gross floor area. Zoning By-law and Official Plan amendment for a new 43-storey mixed-use building on the subject site. The existing 8-and-12-storey student residence will be retained on the subject site, with the northern-most bay of the northern 8-storey portion of that existing student residence being demolished to accommodate the proposed new building. The redevelopment proposes 463 dwelling units, resulting in a total gross floor area (GFA) of 28,998 sq. m., a total of 28,782 sq. m. of residential GFA, and a total of 216 sq. m of retail GFA.

Proposal to fully renovate and partially reconstruct the existing theatre. The existing historic front (west) facade fronting on Mount Pleasant Road will be retained. Construction plans include a new roof, full/new excavated basement, replacing the north, east and south walls, and full interior renovation. A new, 3 storey building addition to the rear (east) is proposed of approximately 1,076 sqm, which includes back of house and new loading area. A small addition is also proposed at the front of the building to create a third floor seating and lobby area. No parking spaces are proposed.
Site Plan Application for proposed four mixed-use buildings ranging from 6 to 36-storeys. Concurrent Draft Plan of Subdivision file reference 21 120673 ESC 24 SB & 21 120678 ESC 24 OZ.

Combined Official Plan & Zoning By-law Amendment for a 56-storey mixed-use building. The proposed building contains a total of 579 dwelling units, of which 36 are rental replacement units, and a total of approximately 4,739 square metres of institutional space for the Garry Hurvitz Centre for Community Mental Health at SickKids located in 440 Jarvis Street.

Proposed a mixed-use development  comprised of five towers ranging in height between 26- to 46-storeys and an 11-storey mid-rise building. Overall 1,881 new residential units, including 165 purpose-built rental units and 341 units for seniors,  a new daycare space that is proposed to be integrated at the base of a rental apartment building. The proposal also includes a range of other non-residential uses including a 228 room hotel as well as a new 687-square metre grocery store, 1,986 square metres of retail space, and 7,412 square metres of office space, including 1,977 square metres of medical office uses that would be conveniently located within the base of the proposed seniors residence. Total of 158,502 square metres of gross floor area resulting in an overall density of 4.57 FSI.

OPA & Rezoning Application proposes the development of an 6-storey mixed-use building providing residential and commercial use at grade. The building will provide a total of 44 residential units, being a mix of 1-bedroom, 2-bedroom, and 3-bedroom units. The building will also provide one retail unit at grade. A paved access from Krawchuck Lane will be provided, leading to 2 levels of below-grade parking, providing a total of 29 parking spaces; as well as 52 bicycle parking spaces. The proposed development provides a total GFA of 3,860 square metres and a FSI of 4.0.
The current application concerns the hotel component of the project, which includes 35 units at Levels 41-45 of the west tower and 15 units of parking at Level A (including 12 stacked parking units).

To permit a mixed use development that would include one north residential building (stepping up to 15-storeys (47.2 metres)) and one south residential building (stepping up to 17-storeys (53.1 metres)) connected by a podium (4-storeys (12.75 metres)) as well as one office building (8-storeys (32.1 metres)). The proposal would include a total gross floor area of 53,072 square metres, of which 43,736 square metres would be for residential space, 7,730 square metres for office space, 1,225 square metres for retail use, and 372 square metres (381 square metres including the vestibule) for community agency space. This site would contain a total of 535 new residential units, of which 525 would be condominium units and 10 would be Affordable Ownership units. A total of 358 vehicular parking spaces are proposed within a 4-level above grade parking garage that would be located along the rail corridor. In addition, a 868 square metres new public park is proposed.

Revised Application (August 2021) : The revised submission has reduced the tower heights of each tower by 2 storeys, to 25 and 29 storeys (previously 27 and 31 storeys), 483 residential units, 522 vehicular and 393 bike parking spaces, 1 type G and 2, type C loading spaces, and an FSI of 5.0 times, in response to City staff comments. See related Site Plan Application No. 21 198274 ESC 20 SA.Original Application: Bousfields Inc. has submitted an application for a Zoning By-law Amendment to permit 2 new residential buildings in addition to the two existing apartment buildings on the subject property. The proposed buildings would be located in the area currently occupied by an existing 4-storey parking structure, which would be demolished. The proposed residential towers of 27 storeys (west tower) and 31 storeys (east tower), would be located above a 6-storey building base (podium). The proposal would have a total of 520 residential units comprising of 200 units in the west tower; 240 units in the east tower; and 80 units in the 6-storey building base. The proposed gross floor area of the new buildings (64, 664 square metres) and the existing residential buildings (28,548 square metres) would result in a Floor Space Index of 5.5 times the lot. A total of 882 vehicular parking spaces, 422 bicycle parking spaces, 1 type-G and 1 type-C loading spaces would be provided to service the proposed development

5 and 43 Junction Road The revised application seeks approval for the redevelopment of 5 and 43 Junction Road, proposing the construction of six residential towers with heights ranging from 15 to 35 storeys. These towers will feature mid-rise podiums that accommodate residential, commercial, and office spaces. The total gross floor area (GFA) of the site will be 135,767.76 square meters, comprising 126,453.36 square meters of residential GFA and 9,314.40 square meters of non-residential GFA. The development will include 1,882 housing units, offering a mix of one, two, and three-bedroom units. Additionally, a 0.32-hectare park will be created, which includes 1,871.1 square meters of parkland dedication and 1,182.5 square meters of landscaped open space. This park will feature a landscaped and integrated rail berm that serves both as community rail protection and a park amenity.
